title: Prometheus の 404 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article consistently and incorrectly states that common Prometheus query issues (e.g., metric not found, no data due to target down, data beyond retention) result in an HTTP 404 Not Found error from the Prometheus Query API.
Prometheus official documentation indicates that these scenarios typically return an HTTP 400 Bad Request with `errorType: bad_data` or an empty 200 OK response, rather than a 404.
While the underlying causes and many troubleshooting steps are correctly described, the fundamental error code associated with these problems in the API is misrepresented.
freshness score is below the threshold
required_actions: Revise all instances where an HTTP 404 error is stated for missing metrics, unavailable target data, or querying beyond retention, replacing it with the accurate HTTP 400 Bad Request (`errorType: bad_data`) or an empty 200 OK response, as appropriate.

Adjust the article title and description to accurately reflect the HTTP status codes and error types being discussed, or explicitly focus on scenarios that genuinely yield a 404 (e.g., incorrect API endpoint URL).
Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.

sources.title: Storage - Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus defaults to retaining data for only 15 days.

sources.title: 8.14. Configuring retention policy for Prometheus metrics - Acronis
sources.claim: The command-line flag `--storage.tsdb.retention.time` is used to configure Prometheus's data retention period, for example, `--storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d` for 30 days.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: When querying the Prometheus API, invalid requests with missing or incorrect parameters typically return a 400 Bad Request status code with a JSON error object, including an `errorType` (e.g., `bad_data`) and an `error` message.

sources.title: Increasing Prometheus storage retention - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: Prometheus has a default data retention of 15 days, which can be extended using the `--storage.tsdb.retention.time` flag at startup.

sources.title: How to Configure and Optimize Prometheus Data Retention - Last9
sources.claim: Prometheus manages data retention using the `--storage.tsdb.retention.time` flag, which defines how long on-disk blocks are kept, with a default of 15 days.

sources.title: How long can Prometheus retention be? - Robust Perception
sources.claim: The Prometheus default retention of 15 days is historical and doesn't reflect technical limits, as Prometheus can retain much longer periods of data depending on available storage.

sources.title: How to Build Metric Naming Conventions - OneUptime
sources.claim: Prometheus requires precise metric names, recommending snake_case and discouraging mixed case or other separators to avoid case sensitivity issues.

sources.title: Prometheus storage - by Naresh Kancharla - Medium
sources.claim: The `--storage.tsdb.retention.time` command-line flag configures data retention, which defaults to 15 days.

sources.title: Knowledge Base - Confluence
sources.claim: The default Prometheus data retention is 15 days, and it can be changed using the `--storage.tsdb.retention.time` flag (e.g., `1y` for one year or `365d` for 365 days).

sources.title: Metric and label naming | Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus metric names must comply with its data model for valid characters and should include a single-word application prefix, with metric type and unit suffixes highly recommended.

sources.title: Prometheus metric naming recommendations - Chronosphere Documentation
sources.claim: Prometheus metric names must follow specific requirements, including using valid characters and often including a prefix relevant to the domain and unit suffixes in plural form.

sources.title: On the naming of things – Robust Perception | Prometheus Monitoring Experts
sources.claim: Prometheus favors snake_case for metric names, and using different casing like camelCase will result in distinct metrics.

sources.title: How to Create Prometheus Retention Strategies - OneUptime
sources.claim: Prometheus uses a time-series database (TSDB) that stores data in 2-hour blocks, which are compacted and deleted based on configured retention settings, with `--storage.tsdb.retention.time` being a key configuration option.

sources.title: Target Scrape Status - Prometheus Trainings by PromLabs
sources.claim: The Prometheus Web UI's 'Status' -> 'Targets' page displays the scrape health state ('UP', 'DOWN', 'UNKNOWN') for all active targets, along with `Last Scrape` and `Last Error` information to debug scrape failures.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: The `/api/v1` endpoint is the stable HTTP API for Prometheus, used for querying and managing metrics.

sources.title: How to Retrieve All Prometheus Metrics - A Step-by-Step Guide | SigNoz
sources.claim: The Prometheus HTTP API, particularly the `/api/v1/query` endpoint, is the primary interface for querying collected metrics.

sources.title: Target Down - kube-prometheus runbooks
sources.claim: If a Prometheus scrape target is down, metrics from that specific target cannot be scraped and will not be available in Prometheus.

sources.title: Excluding down targets in Prometheus - Puppeteers Oy
sources.claim: When a Prometheus scrape target is down, Prometheus will record the 'up' metric for that target as 0, and no other metrics will be received from it.

sources.title: Prometheus Prometheus not scraping all targets - Stack Diagnosis - DrDroid
sources.claim: If Prometheus is not scraping all configured targets due to them being down or misconfigured, it can result in missing metrics or incomplete data.

sources.title: Exporting and scraping Prometheus metrics - GitHub Enterprise Server 3.17 Docs
sources.claim: The `/metrics` endpoint is a standard HTTP endpoint where applications expose their Prometheus-compatible metrics, which can be directly queried using `curl`.

sources.title: TargetDown · prometheus-operator/kube-prometheus Wiki - GitHub
sources.claim: The `/targets` page in the Prometheus UI can be used to check the scrape error for a particular target, and `up == 0` query can check the trend over time for target health.

sources.title: Prometheus Query API - Robusta documentation
sources.claim: The Prometheus Query API (`/api/v1/query`) allows running PromQL queries, and a basic query like `query=up` can be used to test its functionality.

sources.title: api/v1/query requires time · Issue #1460 - GitHub
sources.claim: The Prometheus API can return `errorType: bad_data` for invalid query parameters.

sources.title: query_range and query API return `errorType` code · Issue #6413 - GitHub
sources.claim: The Prometheus API uses `errorType` (e.g., `bad_data`) in its error responses.

sources.title: Prometheus curl API query issue - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: A `curl` command with `-g` can be used to query the Prometheus API with complex PromQL expressions, for example `curl -g 'http://127.0.0.1:20001/api/v1/query?query=max_over_time(container_memory_usage_bytes{pod_name=~"somepod-.*"}[1d])'`.

sources.title: Prometheus Monitoring: From Zero to Hero, The Right Way - Dash0
sources.claim: The Prometheus Web UI (typically on port 9090) includes a 'Status' menu with a 'Targets' page to check scrape target status.

sources.title: intro-to-prometheus-breakouts/breakout-1-exploring-the-prometheus-ui.md at main - GitHub
sources.claim: The Prometheus Web UI provides a 'Status' menu, which includes a 'Targets' page to show configured scrape jobs and their 'up' or 'down' status.

sources.title: How to get metrics starting with particular words in Prometheus and what to update in prometheus.yml file - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: Using `curl` to query Prometheus API for metrics like `up` is a common debugging technique, for instance: `curl 'localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=up'`.

sources.title: List Prometheus Scraping Targets - Thomas Stringer
sources.claim: The `/api/v1/targets` endpoint can be queried to see active scrape targets and their health status (e.g., 'up' or 'down'), `lastError`, and `scrapeUrl`.

sources.title: Setup Prometheus and Grafana for monitoring Gravitino - Gravitino
sources.claim: You can inspect metrics exposed by a target by directly accessing its `/metrics` endpoint with `curl` (e.g., `curl http://localhost:8080/metrics`).

sources.title: PromQL HTTP API | Elasticsearch Reference
sources.claim: Client errors (HTTP 4xx) when using the PromQL API often return `errorType: bad_data` for invalid requests.
